auto_rickshaw_cng.source: ICCT India, Bharat Stage VI Emission Standards and Vehicle Efficiency study (2020)

metro.source: DMRC Sustainability Report 2022-23 (per passenger-km, Scope 2 grid emissions included)

bus_public.source: India GHG Inventory 2016, Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change; per passenger-km on a loaded urban bus

taxi_petrol.source: ICCT India road vehicle emission factors (2023); petrol sedan, per vehicle-km

taxi_cng.source: ICCT India road vehicle emission factors (2023); CNG sedan, per vehicle-km

two_wheeler_petrol.source: ICCT India two-wheeler emission and efficiency study (2023); 125cc-class petrol scooter

two_wheeler_electric.source: ICCT India EV lifecycle analysis (2023) combined with CEA CO2 Baseline Database Version 19.0 (2023-24)
two_wheeler_electric.notes: Assumes Indian national average grid factor of 0.82 kg CO2e/kWh (CEA 2023); actual emissions vary by state. Efficiency basis: 57 Wh/km (typical 125cc-class EV scooter, e.g. Ola S1, Ather 450X).

four_wheeler_petrol.source: ICCT India road vehicle emission factors (2023); B/C-segment petrol passenger car, per vehicle-km

four_wheeler_electric.source: ICCT India EV lifecycle analysis (2023) combined with CEA CO2 Baseline Database Version 19.0 (2023-24)
four_wheeler_electric.notes: Assumes Indian national average grid factor of 0.82 kg CO2e/kWh (CEA 2023); actual emissions vary by state. Efficiency basis: 165 Wh/km (typical B-segment EV, e.g. Tata Nexon EV).

walking.source: Zero by definition; no fuel or grid energy consumed during walking
walking.notes: Zero tailpipe emissions by definition; upstream caloric food emissions are excluded and not modelled here.

wfh.source: Zero by definition; work-from-home involves no commute distance
wfh.notes: Work-from-home transport leg is zero km; home energy consumption is logged separately as an electricity activity type.
